steps.title: Use the shape of the data
steps.content: The plotted data increase at a roughly steady rate, so a linear model is more appropriate than an exponential model. That eliminates choices C and D.

steps.title: Use the direction of the trend
steps.content: Because $y$ increases as $x$ increases, the slope must be positive. Choice B — $y = 694 - 1.2x$ has slope $-1.2$, so it goes the wrong direction.

steps.title: Choose the remaining model
steps.content: Choice A — $y = -694 + 1.2x$, $y=-694+1.2x$, is the only remaining equation with a positive linear trend. That matches the graph, so A is correct.
